name: Cousino & Weinzimmer LLC
description_short: Cousino & Weinzimmer LLC is a bankruptcy law firm in Columbus, OH specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy filings, foreclosure defense, and debt relief for consumers in Ohio.
description_long:
Cousino & Weinzimmer LLC is a bankruptcy law practice based in Columbus, Ohio, with additional service areas in Dayton and Cincinnati. The firm was founded to provide experienced legal representation to consumers navigating financial distress, with a stated mission of guiding clients through bankruptcy with compassion and clarity. The firm serves clients throughout the Southern District of Ohio.

The firm offers comprehensive bankruptcy services including Chapter 7 bankruptcy (debt discharge), Chapter 13 bankruptcy (debt reorganization and repayment plans), foreclosure defense, loan modifications, and loss mitigation programs. They provide free bankruptcy evaluations and claim to help clients eliminate debt and avoid wage garnishment. The firm positions Chapter 7 as a "fresh start" designed to discharge overwhelming debt while protecting essential assets through exemptions, and Chapter 13 as a consolidation mechanism that allows clients to keep homes and vehicles while catching up on missed payments.

Cousino & Weinzimmer distinguishes itself through personalized service with named attorneys (Courtney A. Cousino and Danielle R. Weinzimmer) handling cases directly rather than paralegals. Client testimonials emphasize attorney availability, knowledge, and compassionate approach. The firm advertises hands-on representation and appears to market itself on long-term client relationships and accessibility.

This is a legitimate bankruptcy law firm with no apparent red flags based on available information. However, as with any bankruptcy service, clients should understand that filing bankruptcy has significant long-term credit consequences and should only be pursued after consultation with qualified legal counsel. The website provides no pricing information, bar status verification, or detailed attorney credentials, which clients should verify independently before engagement.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Offers free bankruptcy evaluations to assess eligibility and options
Named attorneys (Courtney A. Cousino and Danielle R. Weinzimmer) provide direct representation rather than paralegals handling cases
Covers multiple geographic areas (Columbus, Dayton, Cincinnati) in Southern District of Ohio
Provides both Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 bankruptcy options tailored to individual circumstances
Offers integrated foreclosure defense and loan modification services alongside bankruptcy filings
Multiple client testimonials emphasize attorney availability and responsiveness
Website includes bankruptcy FAQ and educational resources to help clients understand the process
cons: Website contains no pricing or cost information; consumers must contact firm directly for fee quotes
No verification of attorney bar status, credentials, or disciplinary history provided on website
Limited detail on success rates, case outcomes, or typical timelines for bankruptcy discharge
No information about payment plans or whether firm accepts clients who cannot afford upfront legal fees
Bankruptcy filing has severe long-term credit consequences that the marketing materials downplay in favor of 'fresh start' messaging
best_for: Homeowners facing foreclosure who want to explore Chapter 13 repayment plans to keep their property
Consumers with overwhelming unsecured debt (credit cards, medical bills) seeking Chapter 7 discharge in Ohio
Individuals experiencing wage garnishment or creditor collection actions who need legal protection
Ohio residents who prefer direct attorney representation over large firm paralegal-based bankruptcy services
diagnosis: Cousino & Weinzimmer is appropriate for Ohio consumers facing significant financial distress who need direct attorney representation for Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy, particularly homeowners at risk of foreclosure. Prospective clients should independently verify the firm's bar status and licensing, obtain a detailed fee quote, and understand that bankruptcy, while providing debt relief, will substantially damage credit for 7-10 years and should only be pursued after consultation with qualified counsel.
services: Chapter 13 bankruptcy filing and repayment plan setup
Automatic Stay protection guidance (legal hold on creditor collection)
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
